metadata.match_reason: JD asks for 2-5 yrs with LLMs, RAG, vector DBs (Pinecone/Weaviate/FAISS), LangChain/LlamaIndex.  ScreenSense AI + Whatfix Mirror is flagship GenAI product.
metadata.notes: Bengaluru HQ; JD nearly mirrors Amit's resume; 5-day in-office policy.

research_summary: Whatfix is a $270M-funded DAP platform (Series E) racing toward IPO with AI products targeted at 20% of revenue by 2026. Their flagship AI work: ScreenSense (context+intent AI engine), Seek (enterprise AI agent -- first to complete Salesforce Trailhead challenges), and a new 0-to-1 Enterprise Search product. The Enterprise Search role requires RAG, vector search (BERT/Sentence Transformers), Elasticsearch, and LLM integration -- a direct match to Amit's Milvus/FAISS/LangChain stack. Email pattern: first.last@whatfix.com (88.2%). 9 contacts identified across senior AI/ML, junior ML, and TA roles.
jd_full_text:
Software Engineer E5 - Enterprise Search

Bengaluru, Karnataka, India | Engineering | Full-time

Who are we?

Whatfix is an AI platform advancing the 'userization' of enterprise applications, empowering companies to maximize the ROI of their digital investments. Technology needs adoption. It's no different for AI. As AI reshapes roles, workflows, and human-machine interactions, it also introduces new layers of complexity and user friction. This is where Whatfix plays a pivotal role. A decade old DNA of empowering people to succeed with technology and not replacing them. We call this philosophy Userization: the belief that technology must adapt to the user, not the other way around.

At the heart of userization philosophy is ScreenSense, our proprietary AI engine, which continuously interprets both the context of what users are doing in an application or an AI tool and the intent behind their actions. By combining these signals, Whatfix delivers real-time guidance, nudges, knowledge, and automation directly in the flow of work.

This intelligence powers our entire product suite.

Digital Adoption helps users get productive faster.
Product Analytics uncovers friction and closes adoption gaps.
Mirror allows employees to train in safe, simulated environments.

These are embedded with Whatfix AI Agents which supercharge creation, insights, and user guidance.

Our upcoming AI-first products are already creating a buzz in the market.

Seek is an AI-native assistant that not only knows your business context but can also act across applications to get work done on your behalf.
Whatfix Mirror 2.0 is the world's only System plus Role simulation with a complete assessment to lead the Gen AI simulation category.

About the Role

We are looking for a passionate and experienced Software Engineer (E5 / E6 level) to join our Enterprise Search team, which is at the core of redefining how users discover and interact with information across Whatfix's digital adoption platform. This is a unique opportunity to solve deep information retrieval and search relevance challenges using scalable infrastructure, cutting-edge NLP, and Generative AI.

As an engineer at this level, you'll be expected to operate with strong ownership, lead cross-team technical initiatives, and influence design choices that directly impact user experience and business outcomes.

What You'll Do

Build a 0-to-1 Enterprise Search product with a strong focus on scalability, performance, and relevance.
Lead proof-of-concept efforts to validate ideas quickly and align with business goals.
Architect and implement robust, maintainable, and scalable systems for indexing, querying, and ranking.
Develop data pipelines, implement automation for reliability, and ensure strong observability and monitoring.
Work closely with Product Managers and Designers to translate user needs into data-driven, intuitive search experiences.
Guide and support junior engineers through code reviews, technical direction, and best practices.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams (data, platform, infra) to deliver cohesive and high-impact solutions.

What We're Looking For

Must-Have Skills:

Familiarity with LLMs, RAG pipelines, or knowledge graph integrations.
Deep expertise in information retrieval, search engines (Lucene, Elasticsearch, Solr).
Experience with vector search, embeddings, and/or neural ranking models (e.g., BERT, Sentence Transformers).
Strong programming skills in Java, Python, or Go.
Familiarity with scalable data processing frameworks (e.g., Spark, Kafka, Flink).
Good understanding of system design, APIs, caching, and performance tuning.

Nice-to-Have:

Experience with enterprise content connectors (SharePoint, Confluence, Jira, etc.).
Experience working in a SaaS, B2B, or product-first environment.

Qualifications

6-10+ years of experience building backend systems, infrastructure, or AI platforms at scale.
Proven ability to own and deliver complex features independently, collaborate across teams, and mentor peers in a fast-paced environment.
Demonstrated experience leading initiatives with significant technical and organizational impact.
tailoring_notes: Summary rewritten to lead with production retrieval and enterprise search -- JD is Enterprise Search E5, ScreenSense AI engine, information retrieval and search relevance. Skills reordered: Vector DBs and Retrieval first (Milvus, BGE-M3, SPLADE -- core search infra), LLMs 2nd, Languages 3rd, Infrastructure 4th; Hardware/IoT dropped. IIT Delhi trimmed to 1 bullet. Projects: WeepScope replaced with SAP Analytics Bot.

contacts.contact_role: Engineering Manager, Data Science & Product Analytics
contacts.research_hook: Recently shared a Whatfix job posting for Lead Software Engineer E5/E6 - Analytics on LinkedIn -- he is actively hiring for his AI/Analytics team. His domain (product analytics + data science) is adjacent to the Enterprise Search embedding and behavioral signal work.
contacts.subject: question about Whatfix's embedding stack
contacts.content:
Hi Abhishek,

Saw you posted the Lead SWE E5/E6 Analytics role on LinkedIn recently. You're building out the analytics team fast, and the behavioral signal work feeding into product analytics sits pretty close to what I do on the retrieval side: embedding pipelines, intent classification, query rewriting.

Quick context: I spent the last year and a half running RAG infrastructure in production. At Silver Touch, I led a full vector DB evaluation on 50K+ queries/month, tested 6 embedding models including BGE-M3 and SPLADE, and rebuilt retrieval from scratch. MRR went 45% to 95%. At Vivansh right now, building a permission-aware agentic system with LangGraph.

I noticed the Enterprise Search opening. 0-to-1 build with RAG and vector search at the core, which is exactly what I've been doing. If your team is hiring on the search or analytics-ML side and a quick call makes sense, I'd take it.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: question about Whatfix's embedding stack
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Abhishek,

Quick follow-up since I last wrote. I put together a short comparison of BGE-M3 vs SPLADE retrieval behavior on a domain-specific corpus, similar to what an enterprise knowledge base would look like. Happy to send it over if it's useful context.

Still curious whether the search or analytics-ML track at Whatfix has room for someone with this background.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: question about Whatfix's embedding stack
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Abhishek,

Last note from me. If Whatfix isn't actively hiring on the ML/search side right now, totally fine. Either way, good luck with the IPO prep.

Amit

contacts.contact_role: Engineering Leader, Full Stack + Gen AI, Whatfix AI
contacts.research_hook: Leads the Whatfix AI product engineering pillar (Guidance, Bizapps, Whatfix AI, Success) for 3+ years. Directly owns ScreenSense and Seek engineering. Her Gen AI + Full Stack leadership role makes her the right person to reach for the Enterprise Search AI track.
contacts.subject: Whatfix Seek + the retrieval layer
contacts.content:
Hi Kanchan,

Seek completing the Trailhead Admin Challenges in October was a meaningful milestone. An AI agent that can navigate and complete certification workflows end-to-end means the retrieval and context layer is working at a level most agents don't reach.

I've been building that retrieval layer for the past two years. At Silver Touch: 50K+ queries/month, a full migration from FAISS to Milvus, 6 embedding models evaluated on production data, latency cut from 16s to 8s via intent classification upfront. At Vivansh now: a 2-stage permission-aware system with 250+ granular access filters per query using LangGraph.

The Enterprise Search opening caught my eye. 0-to-1 with RAG and vector search is the exact kind of build I want to be part of. If you're filling that role and a 15-minute conversation would help, I'm around.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: Whatfix Seek + the retrieval layer
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Kanchan,

Following up. Since I wrote last, I read ScreenSense's design notes from the February launch -- context plus intent inference is a harder problem than most teams realize, and the way you've scoped it around application-layer signals is genuinely interesting.

Still interested in the Enterprise Search role if you're building the team.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: Whatfix Seek + the retrieval layer
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Kanchan,

Last note. If the timing or profile isn't right, no problem. Good luck with the AI product roadmap this year.

Amit

contacts.research_hook: IIT-educated Staff Engineer at Whatfix working on core platform. Staff-level engineers at this scale typically shape the architecture of new product tracks like Enterprise Search -- strong technical peer to reach.
contacts.subject: Enterprise Search architecture at Whatfix
contacts.content:
Hi Gaurav,

Going through Whatfix's Enterprise Search JD this week. A 0-to-1 search product with Elasticsearch at the center and RAG layered on top is the kind of problem I've spent the last two years working on.

At Silver Touch, I built retrieval infrastructure for a multi-tenant chatbot at 50K+ queries/month. That meant choosing between vector DBs (FAISS vs Qdrant vs Milvus), running embedding benchmarks across 6 candidates on production corpora, and designing a 2-stage retrieval setup that got MRR to 95%. Not Elasticsearch-native, but the same indexing and ranking tradeoffs apply.

Figured it's worth asking: is the team actively interviewing for that role, and what does the technical bar look like for E5? If not the right time, no worries.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | github.com/amit-gohel | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: Enterprise Search architecture at Whatfix
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Gaurav,

One more thought since I last wrote. I've been working on a query rewriting layer using intent classification before the retrieval step -- cuts latency significantly on ambiguous queries. That's the kind of optimization I'd expect to matter a lot in enterprise search where queries are rarely clean.

Still curious about the team and the role.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: Enterprise Search architecture at Whatfix
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Gaurav,

Last note from me. If the role or timing doesn't fit, totally fine.

Amit

contacts.research_hook: ML engineer at Whatfix with NLP focus, TU Munich + NIT Surathkal background. His headline: 'ML @ Whatfix | ML, NLP | TUM | NIT S'. Amit's semantic retrieval and NLP pipeline work (document parsing, embedding pipelines) maps directly to what Rohit builds day-to-day.
contacts.subject: NLP at Whatfix - quick question
contacts.content:
Hi Rohit,

Your TUM ML background plus NLP work at Whatfix caught my attention. I'm an ML engineer who's been working on semantic retrieval and NLP pipelines -- document parsing, embedding evaluation, query preprocessing.

At Silver Touch, I ran a benchmark of 6 embedding models (BGE-M3, BGE small/base/large, SPLADE, Stella) on a production document corpus, then built a custom retrieval setup with BGE-M3 plus a cross-encoder reranker. A lot of the interesting work was figuring out where sparse retrieval (SPLADE) outperformed dense retrieval and where it didn't.

I'm looking at the Enterprise Search opening at Whatfix. Is it a team you know well, and would you say the NLP/ML component of the role is a significant part of the day-to-day work?

Amit
linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: NLP at Whatfix - quick question
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Rohit,

Following up. I put some notes together on sparse vs dense retrieval tradeoffs I've seen on enterprise document corpora. Happy to share if it's useful context.

And still curious whether the Enterprise Search ML work is a good fit from your vantage point.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: NLP at Whatfix - quick question
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Rohit,

Last note from me. If it's not the right moment, no worries.

Amit

contacts.subject: product engineering at Whatfix
contacts.content:
Hi Priyangshu,

Was reading about Whatfix's Enterprise Search role this week. The product engineering angle -- building a 0-to-1 search experience on top of RAG infrastructure -- is the kind of work I want to move into.

I'm currently an AI engineer at Vivansh Infotech: LangGraph-based agentic RAG with 250+ permission filters per query, running 1,000+ queries/day. Before that, Silver Touch -- rebuilt a multi-tenant chatbot at 50K+ queries/month, ran the vector DB and embedding model evaluation from scratch.

Would you say the product engineering culture at Whatfix is a good fit for someone with a heavier ML/backend profile? And is the search team actively interviewing?

The Enterprise Search E5 role caught my attention specifically -- RAG plus vector search is exactly what I've been building. Resume and portfolio: amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai.
